title: Finding the Perimeter of a Rectangle

steps.instruction: A rectangle has a length of 10 cm and a width of 4 cm. List all sides.
steps.work: Top: 10 cm, Right: 4 cm, Bottom: 10 cm, Left: 4 cm.
steps.explanation: A rectangle has 2 pairs of equal sides: 2 lengths and 2 widths.

steps.instruction: Add the side lengths to find the perimeter.
steps.explanation: Perimeter is the total distance around the shape.
For rectangles, you can use this shortcut instead of adding all 4 sides.

title: Finding the Perimeter of an Irregular Shape

steps.instruction: An L-shaped room has sides: 5 m, 3 m, 2 m, 4 m, 3 m, 7 m. Find the perimeter.
steps.work: The shape has 6 sides with different lengths.
steps.explanation: Since the sides are not all equal, we must add each one individually.

steps.instruction: Add the lengths of all 6 sides.
steps.explanation: Go around the shape and add each side, making sure not to miss any.
For irregular shapes, the only method is to add all side lengths. There's no multiplication shortcut.
